records.ety: [OF. tens, properly, time, F. temps time, tense. See Temporal of time, and cf. Thing.]

defs: One of the forms which a verb takes by inflection or by adding auxiliary words, so as to indicate the time of the action or event signified; the modification which verbs undergo for the indication of time.
notes: ☞ The primary simple tenses are three: those which express time past, present, and future; but these admit of modifications, which differ in different languages.

records.ety: [L. tensus, p. p. of tendere to stretch. See Tend to move, and cf. Toise.]

defs: Stretched tightly; strained to stiffness; rigid; not lax; as, a tense fiber.
quotes: The temples were sunk, her forehead was tense, and a fatal paleness was upon her.
